title: Manually Generate "filter" URL
excerpt:
When I filter a board using the UI, I see a URL generated in this format: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done… 
What is the encoding used for this “filter” variable? Can I use the same format to create filtered URLs on…

posts.author: mittalvishesh (Vish)
posts.cookedHtml:
When I filter a board using the UI, I see a URL generated in this format: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done…

What is the encoding used for this “filter” variable? Can I use the same format to create filtered URLs on my own?

Use Case: We have an internal line of business application. We want to embed URLs that redirect our users to specific filtered Monday board depending on which record they are viewing in our application.

Any help is much appreciated.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mittalvishesh

That’s a great first post! My warmest welcome to the community and I hope you enjoy your stay with us

Just to make sure we’re on the same page here, are you looking for a way to input those URLs manually, without using the monday.com interface? Does using filters and then copying the URL work for you in general?

I’d love to understand the use case and need here a little bit better, so that we could provide the most relevant advice here

-Alex

posts.author: mittalvishesh (Vish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alex

Thanks for your reply. We have a board for “Accounts”. Each “Account” has a lot of “Tasks”. Those tasks are listed on a separate Monday board.

From the Monday UI, I can easily see the tasks for one account by going to the “Tasks” board and using the filter to filter for one Account. Straightforward so far.

Our external line of business app needs to generate such filtered URLs for every account. So if we can understand the format of these URLs, we can programmatically generate these URLs for every account one by one.

posts.author: mittalvishesh (Vish)
posts.cookedHtml:
For example, if the URL was of this format:
https://example.com/board/filter?field1=12123121, I can see that changing the “12123121” to some other value will filter the board differently.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mittalvishesh

Got it, thank you so much for clarifying! To be transparent with you, I’m not quite sure if this would be possible to achieve programmatically, but I’ll check in with our developers just to be sure.

On the other hand, it might also make sense to create a Board View/Dashboard Widget app that would do the filtering for you, based on the logic you create within the app directly. This will mean you will have full control over the way the filtering works, although it also means that you’ll need to develop this solution.

As soon as I have more insight to share on this, I’ll let you know.

-Alex

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mittalvishesh

After careful consideration with the product team, I’m afraid that this is indeed not possible at this time. That said, I’ve passed the feature request along to our product team and perhaps this is something we will be able to implement in the future.

Thanks so much for your patience here! As of today, I’ll also move the topic to the feature request section to allow other users to vote for the feature.

-Alex

posts.author: easynerdy (Mark Berry)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like this feature as well!

posts.author: JonOfAllTrades (Jonathan Shaltz)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would make it possible to store a bookmark to a board which includes filters. It’s very annoying having to set my preferred filters every time I pull up Monday.com.

posts.author: TSA (Andrea Fantini)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alex
Has this request been considered to date? I find it very useful and interesting in order to integrate Monday in various usage modes and thus increase sales potential.
Can you provide updates or possible solutions?
Thank you.

posts.author: FrikPR (Frik vC)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been doing this - setting up filters and then bookmarking the URL. So tomorrow when I come back and click the link, everything is filtered already and I continue working.
HOWEVER…. I am here today looking around because for about two weeks now this isn’t working anymore…. Not sure what has happened to Monday.
